cit.basis: Income Tax Act 2023; FY2024-25 rates administered by the National Board of Revenue (NBR).

conditions.banking_channel_note: Non-listed companies qualify for a reduced 25% rate on condition that all receipts and expenses are transacted via the banking channel (bank transfer); cash transactions forfeit the concession.
conditions.listed_low_float_note: Listed companies that floated less than 10% of paid-up capital via IPO are taxed at 25% instead of 22.5%.

minimum_tax.note: Minimum tax of 0.6% on gross receipts may apply irrespective of profit or loss — out of scope for this SME estimator.

cit.note: Banks, insurance companies and NBFIs: 37.5% (listed) / 40% (non-listed). Mobile phone operators and tobacco manufacturers: 45%.

vat.note: VAT & Supplementary Duty Act 2012: 15% standard rate with reduced 5% / 7.5% / 10% bands for specific goods and services; returns filed via Mushak forms.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year FY2024-25 (Bangladesh income year 1 July 2024 – 30 June 2025); jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Banking-channel condition, minimum tax on gross receipts, surcharges, WHT, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
